title: Resources and Community
text: Badger Biodata works closely alongside BRUG to share resources and collaborate. One of the resposibilities of Badger Biodata is to participate in BRUG’s meetings and contributing to BRUG’s website.

title: Badger Biodata Highlights
text: Highlight is incoming after the first meeting!

title: About The Badger Biodata Initiative
text:
Badger Biodata creates a space upon campus to learn, create, and share about bioinformatics, bio-computation, and biological data projects.
Our goal is to support member projects, create a club portfolio, boost member expereince, and create a network on campus.

section: The Badger Biodata Initiative Mission Statement
text:
The Badger Biodata Initiative Mission Statement
Badger Biodata Initiative is directed to those interested in programming projects related to biology and biological data. The goal is to create a space to organize groups of members to collaborate, create notable projects, learn together, and for each member to gain experience. Our promise to each member is to allow for the opportunity to learn and gain experience with programming languages, git, github, and useful libraries.

What we do

Build Biological Data Projects
Learn & Use Industry-Relevant Tools
Explore Bio-computational and Bioinformatical Concepts
Establish Connections and Collaborate

How to Join
Interested applications should fill out the Badger Biodata Membership Form & Agreement. Members will be processed and reached out to via our email.
Join our Official Badger Biodata Google Slack Group!

title: Member Projects
text:
CNN Model Analyzing non-coding DNA for Cancer
Using Supervised training for a CNN to analyze large DNA datasets, specifically the non-coding DNA sequences, to discover possible patterns that result in Melanomia cancer.
Author(s): Jayden Merrick
Github link
